chaptername: 026 Attaining Buddhahood through Qi Entering the Central Channel
chapterbody: n this article, I will briefly explain how the qi enters the central channel, enabling one to reach the state of Buddhahood. Please read all sentences carefully because each sentence is a key.
chapterbody 3: First, one guides qi into the central channel, which transforms the expedient qi into wisdom qi. One visualizes one’s principal deity at the brow-point.
chapterbody 4: (Hold the qi for a period of time, visualize one’s principal deity rising and manifesting.)
chapterbody 6: Second, the practitioner becomes the principal deity.
chapterbody 7: (At this point, the practitioner shows divine pride and focuses on the principal deity.)
chapterbody 9: Third, the principal deity descends to the navel chakra. At this moment, one chants a mantra silently and performs the Treasure Vase Breathing Practice.
chapterbody 10: One guides the “Ascending Qi” and “Descending Qi” to gather at the navel chakra. One uses the techniques of squeezing, pressing, and rubbing to ignite one’s inner fire.
chapterbody 11: Squeezing. (To accumulate qi)
chapterbody 12: Pressing. (To hold tightly)
chapterbody 13: Rubbing. (Friction)
chapterbody 14: These are important key points, which can cause one’s inner fire to ignite.
chapterbody 16: Fourth, the inner fire rises to the ajna chakra and dissolves the white light drops. The white light drops descend along the central channel, which evokes the feeling of the Four Blisses.
chapterbody 17: The preliminary bliss. (As the white light drops enter the throat chakra)
chapterbody 18: The excellent bliss. (As the white light drops enter the heart chakra)
chapterbody 19: The superior bliss. (As the white light drops enter the navel chakra)
chapterbody 20: The absolute bliss (As the white light drops enter the sacral chakra)
chapterbody 22: Fifth, one reverses the flow of the light drops along the central channel back to the ajna chakra, resulting in the Four Levels of Emptiness. These Four Levels of Emptiness also include the feeling of the Four Blisses.
chapterbody 23: (The simultaneous sensations present the bliss and emptiness as nondual and yet not distinct.)
chapterbody 25: Sixth, one visualizes the principal deity at one’s navel chakra with a physical or an imagined consort, the white light drops within the firm vajra scepter and the red light drops at the crown chakra result in a feeling of bliss.
chapterbody 26: (This evokes the feeling of bliss)
chapterbody 28: Seventh, one accumulates all the white and red light drops in the central channel, layer by layer like a multi-layered cake.
chapterbody 29: As long as a small amount of light drops are gathered together, a small amount of bliss will be generated, which will eliminate some karmic hindrances.
chapterbody 30: The practitioner achieves Buddhahood through the manifestation of the principal deity.
chapterbody 31: (At this point, the red and white light drops have accumulated completely inside the central channel.)
chapterbody 33: Pay attention to these steps:
chapterbody 35: The principal deity and the consort of the principal deity become unified. The inner fire dissolves the white light drops at the ajna chakra, causing the white light drops to descend to the tip of the vajra scepter. This evokes the magnificent feeling of the Four Blisses.
chapterbody 37: Then, one reverses the flow of the light drops along the central channel back to the ajna chakra. This evokes the extraordinary feeling of the Four Blisses and Four Levels of Emptiness.
chapterbody 38: The white light drops mentioned here are referring to the body’s secretion of hormones. The red light drops are referring to the inner fire.
chapterbody 39: The emptiness is the illusory body.
chapterbody 40: The form is the light.
chapterbody 42: My personal experience related to this practice is as follows:
chapterbody 43: Qi entering into the central channel is essential.
chapterbody 44: Igniting the inner fire is essential.
chapterbody 45: Dissolving the light drops is essential.
chapterbody 46: Descending of the light drops is essential.
chapterbody 47: Ascending of the light drops is essential.
chapterbody 48: Opening of the five chakras is essential.
chapterbody 49: Manifestation of the principal deity is essential.
chapterbody 50: Unification of emptiness and form is essential.
chapterbody 51: (Unification of the illusory body and pure light is the state of Buddhahood.)
chapterbody 53: “In this article, why don’t you mention that the father-mother practice is essential? Since the father-mother practice can cause the inner fire to ascend, and the light drops to descend, why don’t you mention it?”
chapterbody 54: My response is:
chapterbody 55: Humans are the most troublesome beings.
chapterbody 56: Someone may ask me:
chapterbody 58: “Human beings are the most troublesome creatures. If the father-mother practice is mentioned, these creatures will create a clamor.”

chapterbody: enerally, a protection practice occurs at the end of the Preliminary Practices. But, when necessary in daily life, the practice can be performed independently. Guarding oneself and one’s residence is very important.
chapterbody 3: I have taught everyone:
chapterbody 4: Form the Vajra Mudra.
chapterbody 5: Recite the mantra, “Om, bo-ru-lan-ze, lee.”
chapterbody 6: Bring the Vajra Mudra to touch five different locations: head, throat, heart, left shoulder, and right shoulder.
chapterbody 7: Then, visualize Vajrapani Bodhisattva illuminating oneself; or oneself transforming into Vajrapani Bodhisattva.
chapterbody 8: This is the Armour Protection Method.
chapterbody 10: In addition, there are other methods to guard oneself:
chapterbody 11: In the first method, one chants the personal deity’s heart mantra, and visualizes the deity on top of one’s head transforming into a moon disc.
chapterbody 12: One visualizes the following three consecutive times: the moon disc dissolves into nectar, which descends into the practitioner’s body drop by drop. The practitioner’s body becomes the white color of the moon disc.
chapterbody 13: One then recites, “All phenomena of self and others are originally pure.”
chapterbody 15: In the second method, one visualizes Vairocana Buddha between the eyebrows, Amitabha Buddha in the throat chakra, Akshobhya Buddha in the heart chakra, Ratnasambhava Buddha in the navel chakra, and Amoghasiddhi Buddha in the sacral chakra.
chapterbody 16: One can also visualize the seed syllables of the five buddhas.
chapterbody 17: One then visualizes oneself transforming into Vajrasattva.
chapterbody 18: Then one recites this mantra, “Om, be-dza, sah-do, ah, hum-pei.”
chapterbody 20: In the third method, one recites the karma eradication mantra of the Seven Buddhas. One then cleanses the hands, before visualizing; one’s the right hand transforming into the moon, and the left hand transforming into the sun.
chapterbody 21: Place the palms together, and visualize a blue-green “hum” () syllable appearing within the palms of the sun and moon. The syllable then transforms into a vajra scepter.
chapterbody 22: Touch the head, throat, heart, navel, sacral chakra, both shoulders, and feet with the vajra scepter (the palms).
chapterbody 23: Recites one’s personal deity’s mantra.
chapterbody 24: Visualize oneself and one’s personal deity unified, abiding in the personal deity’s divine pride.
chapterbody 26: In the fourth method, one visualizes oneself transforming into the personal deity. The personal deity’s seed syllables then cover one’s body, and one becomes the personal deity’s original nature of indestructible vajra.
chapterbody 27: One then is protected by the personal deity wearing vajra armor or with powerful weapons.
chapterbody 29: In the fifth method, the practitioner’s own channels, chakras, joints, etc., are all covered with sacred syllables. The syllables transform into vajra scepters.
chapterbody 30: Each vajra scepter emits the five colors, not only shining on oneself, but also shining on all evil obstructions in the ten directions.
chapterbody 31: The evil obstructions in the ten directions are thereby destroyed.
chapterbody 32: Above are the methods of cultivating protection. The key point is transforming the mudras, visualizations, and syllables into wisdom.
chapterbody 34: I would like to share a joke:
chapterbody 35: A man said, “Every time I have a problem with my wife, we end up feeling thirsty.”
chapterbody 36: His friend said, “You two must be swearing and yelling at each other too much!”
chapterbody 37: The man replied, “No, we don’t even say one word.”
chapterbody 38: His friend then asked, “How can that happen?”
chapterbody 39: The man replied, “This is because we just spit on each other!”
chapterbody 40: (Hahaha!)
chapterbody 42: When facing evil obstructions, we do not need to swear nor spit. We just cultivate the practices that protect ourselves. One will then be thoroughly purified.
chapterbody 43: The key points are:
chapterbody 44: Purity.
chapterbody 45: Indestructibility.
chapterbody 46: Firmness.
chapterbody 50: At the end, the practitioner needs to provide offerings to all the spiritual guardians.
chapterbody 52: Sheng-yen Lu

chapterbody: n this chapter, I will continue to discuss the protection practices regarding a residence.
chapterbody 3: In the past, I have taught:
chapterbody 4: The vajra foundation.
chapterbody 5: The vajra wall.
chapterbody 6: The vajra flaming wall.
chapterbody 7: The vajra net.
chapterbody 8: Above is one of the residence protection methods.
chapterbody 10: Another method that I often practice, like every half month. One must put the palms together and recite the Nine-character Mantra.
chapterbody 11: Reciting “Lin, bing, dou, zhe, jie, zhen, lie, zai, qian,” to bless everywhere in the house, cleans and eradicates all evil hindrances, and completely expels all the demons and evil spirits inside the residence.
chapterbody 13: The following is a Tibetan Buddhism practice:
chapterbody 14: Establish an emptiness shrine first;
chapterbody 15: In the middle is a half-moon shape wind shrine;
chapterbody 16: Above is a triangle fire altar.
chapterbody 17: (Three characters of emptiness, wind, and fire.)
chapterbody 19: Subsequently, a protection wheel rises with a foundation below. Five-layered walls then appear.
chapterbody 20: Each protective wall is fully covered with swords and spears to prevent invasions from ghosts and evil spirits.
chapterbody 21: A vajra net suspended in the air protects from above.
chapterbody 22: A vajra foundation is underneath.
chapterbody 23: Vajra walls surround the four sides.
chapterbody 24: Sharp weapons or fire covering the walls.
chapterbody 25: Within the space visualized above is a vajra palace and in the center of the palace is a “dharma throne.” The practitioner is cultivating on a sun-disc pad that is on the lotus dharma throne.
chapterbody 26: (The above visualization is used to protect a residence)
chapterbody 28: One then invokes dharma protectors to descend one by one from the void.
chapterbody 29: The dharma protectors may be:
chapterbody 30: The ten wrathful Wisdom Kings.
chapterbody 31: The ten Removal Protectors.
chapterbody 32: The ten Constellations Deities.
chapterbody 33: The ten Dragon Kings.
chapterbody 34: The ten ghost subjugating deities.
chapterbody 35: These spiritual guardians are invoked to protect the practitioners during their cultivation. Each one of these protectors stands guard around the residence.
chapterbody 36: (The positioning of these protectors is similar to the illustrations in a Mandala.)
chapterbody 38: The practitioner transforms into the personal deity.
chapterbody 39: Throughout these complicated procedures, proper manifestations rely on clear visualization.
chapterbody 41: At the end, the practitioner needs to provide offerings to all the spiritual guardians.
chapterbody 42: These offerings can include purified and blessed meat, liquor, water and many other offerings.
chapterbody 44: Please pay attention. During our dharma ceremonies, we have often photographed prajna lights. If one enlarges the image of these prajna lights and looks closely, one will notice that each prajna light contains a deity in the center and the deity is surrounded by dharma protectors.
chapterbody 45: In other words, every prajna light is a manifestation of a deity’s abode.
chapterbody 47: Moreover, when traveling by air, some practitioners have taken beautiful photographs of their plane surrounded by rainbow lights.
chapterbody 48: The phenomenon is a manifestation of the honorable guardians protecting the practitioners in the aircraft.
chapterbody 50: The key points are:
chapterbody 51: Clear visualization.
chapterbody 52: Correct sadhana.
chapterbody 56: Sincere offerings.
chapterbody 58: All the behaviors and dignity of a practitioner are the pure manifestation from the wisdom of great bliss of the principal deity.

chapterbody: used to teach Dream Yoga this way:
chapterbody 2: Before sleeping, one visualizes a syllable “” (seh) at the throat chakra.
chapterbody 3: The syllable transforms into Amitabha Buddha.
chapterbody 5: Amitabha Buddha emits red light that projects into the void.
chapterbody 6: Like fireworks, the great red light expanding into a magnificent parasol.
chapterbody 7: The red light parasol gradually descends.
chapterbody 8: The practitioner’s body covers by the great red light.
chapterbody 9: One then falls asleep. This practice is one form of Dream Yoga.
chapterbody 11: Alternatively:
chapterbody 12: Before sleeping, one visualizes that Vairocana Buddha is located at one’s ajna chakra, Amitabha Buddha at the throat chakra, Akshobhya Buddha at the heart chakra, Ratnasambhava Buddha at the navel chakra, and Amoghasiddhi Buddha at the sacral chakra.
chapterbody 13: One may also visualize the corresponding seed syllables of the five buddhas respectively at the chakras instead.
chapterbody 14: All the buddhas emit light.
chapterbody 15: The practitioner thus sleeps in the five colors of light. This is also a Dream Yoga.
chapterbody 17: Moreover:
chapterbody 18: Before sleeping, one visualizes a syllable “rang” that sets one’s whole body on fire, burning the body into ashes.
chapterbody 19: One then visualizes a syllable “yang” and the ashes are blown away by the wind. The practitioner’s body becomes like the empty void.
chapterbody 20: Finally, the practitioner visualizes a syllable “kang” and everything disappears.
chapterbody 21: This is equal to a drop of water merging into the sea, or unification of the child light with the mother light, which implies that a small light is assimilated into a big light.
chapterbody 22: This is another form of Dream Yoga.
chapterbody 24: Meanwhile:
chapterbody 25: Upon waking, one visualizes that dakinis are playing damarus that produce dong, dong, dong sounds.
chapterbody 26: One may also visualize goddesses playing celestial music.
chapterbody 27: The dakinis or goddesses persuade the practitioner to emerge from the light meditation.
chapterbody 28: The practitioner transforms into the principal deity.
chapterbody 30: Above are all the keys.
chapterbody 31: In the teachings of Mahayoga:
chapterbody 32: All the behaviors and dignity of a practitioner are the pure manifestation from the wisdom of great bliss of the principal deity.
chapterbody 33: Everyone around the practitioner becomes a heavenly being of the principal deity.
chapterbody 34: The entire outside world transforms into celestial palaces and castles.
chapterbody 35: All visualizations in one’s mind become a mandala.
chapterbody 37: Again, these are all keys.
chapterbody 38: After succeeding in the development stage, we should enter the completion stage.
chapterbody 39: Since the completion stage is supramundane, it enables a practitioner to transform into an enlightened buddha.
chapterbody 41: I will make an analogy by relating a joke:
chapterbody 42: If I were a leader who plans to win the World Cup of Football, I would select a group of twenty-year-old strong young men who have been sentenced to death.
chapterbody 43: I would have them trained to play football for several years.
chapterbody 44: Then I would let them play for the World Cup.
chapterbody 45: If they win, they would be set free.
chapterbody 46: If they lose, they would be executed.
chapterbody 48: Now that it is a game of life and death, I can say that this would guarantee that they will win!
chapterbody 50: I observe that we Buddhist practitioners are the same. We must strive to cultivate.
chapterbody 51: If we are unsuccessful – we remain in the cycle of life and death.
chapterbody 52: If we are successful – we become a buddha.
chapterbody 56: If you are willing to suffer in the six realms of transmigration, again and again going through the life imprisonment, you do not need to cultivate.
chapterbody 58: Through such cultivation, it is very easy for a practitioner to accomplish the Path of Accumulation, attain selflessness, and achieve emptiness.

chapterbody: would like to first share some facts.
chapterbody 2: In Tibet, no matter how hard one tries, it is impossible to boil a pot of water. This shows that one’s “environment” is very important.
chapterbody 3: When riding a bicycle, no matter how hard one tries, it is impossible to catch a motorcycle. This shows that “equal status” is very important.
chapterbody 4: A man, no matter how impressive who he is, without a woman, cannot have a baby. This reveals that “cooperation” is very important.
chapterbody 6: No matter how capable a person is, it is difficult to compete against a group of people. This shows that “teamwork” is very important.
chapterbody 7: If one wants water to drink, no matter how large a bucket, it is not as good as digging a well. This shows that “resources” are very important.
chapterbody 8: If one wants to receive the five blessings, one must believe in cause and effect, pay attention to moral cultivation, be virtuous and bear the utmost onerous duties. This shows that one’s “behavior” is very important.
chapterbody 9: In order to attain achievement, one must sincerely change their actions, thoughts, and mind. This shows that one’s “mentality” is very important.
chapterbody 11: Two frogs fell in love. After getting married, they gave birth to a toad. The father frog was furious. The mother frog cried and said that she had plastic surgery before the marriage. This shows that “understanding” is very important.
chapterbody 13: A young donkey said to an old donkey that we eat grass every day but the dairy cow eats special feed every meal! The old donkey sighed and said that we rely on our legs to make a living, and the cow relies on its udders to make a living. This shows that “conditions” are very important.
chapterbody 15: A duck raced a crab, and at the end it was difficult to tell who the winner was. A referee decided that they should play rock-paper-scissors to determine who would be the winner. The duck was furious, because the duck could only show paper, and the crab would always show scissors. This shows that “innateness” is very important.
chapterbody 17: A dog asked a bear to marry him and promised the bear that she would be happy. The bear replied that if I married you, our offspring would be a black bear. I wanted to marry a cat so that I would have a distinguished panda bear. This shows that “selection” is very important.
chapterbody 19: My purpose of sharing the above is mainly to explain the following:
chapterbody 20: Giving results in the largest provisions. Good fortune comes from charity. In order to receive the greatest blessings, one needs to give completely. What is to give completely? Offering one’s body! This is the greatest blessing. This shows that the practice of body offering is of greatest importance.
chapterbody 22: The practice of body offering is as follows:
chapterbody 23: First, one visualizes one’s own light drop, as big as a bean, flying up to the void. While one recites the syllable “Pei,” the light drop transforms into a Krodha Kali, a black wrathful emanation of Machig Labdron.
chapterbody 24: The Krodha Kali holds a kartika in the right hand, a kapala (skull cap) in the left hand, and a khatvanga in the crook of the left arm.
chapterbody 26: The practitioner lies supine, and visualizes that the body is a corpse as big as the universe.
chapterbody 27: The practitioner then visualizes that the Krodha Kali severs the head of the corpse with the kartika and erects the head on three poles.
chapterbody 28: There is raging fire under the poles.
chapterbody 29: The practitioner’s head transforms into a skull cap.
chapterbody 31: Krodha Kali continues to dismember the corpse with the kartika. While she is burning each piece one by one, all pieces of the corpse become a nectar.
chapterbody 32: The syllable “Hang” becomes a white nectar.
chapterbody 33: The syllable “Ah” becomes a red nectar.
chapterbody 34: The practitioner offers the red and white nectar to one’s guru, principal deity, and dharma protectors first.
chapterbody 35: The practitioner then offers the red and white nectar to the beings in the Six Paths and one’s karmic enemies.
chapterbody 36: This makes the guru, principal deity, and dharma protectors happy.
chapterbody 37: This makes the practitioner’s karmic enemies leave happily. This makes the ghosts and gods happy.
chapterbody 38: (While visualizing, one chants “om-ah-hum,” the three syllable mantra.)
chapterbody 40: This practice teaches one to sacrifice oneself.
chapterbody 41: This practice helps one to develop the bodhicitta.
chapterbody 42: The practitioner completely abandons themselves, and becomes an offering.
chapterbody 43: The practitioner eliminates their greed, anger, ignorance and so on.
chapterbody 45: Through such cultivation, it is very easy for a practitioner to accomplish the Path of Accumulation, attain selflessness, and achieve emptiness.
chapterbody 46: All the keys in this practice are vast and perfect.
chapterbody 47: The biggest giving is the greatest blessing!

chapterbody: n a small room, I first lit a stick of incense before turning off the lights and closing the curtains. The whole room now was completely dark, except for a red spot from the burning incense.
chapterbody 3: I sat in a natural position focusing my eyes on the little red spot of the burning incense.
chapterbody 4: Without deliberately focusing.
chapterbody 5: Neither a scattered mind.
chapterbody 6: I was just looking at the little red spot naturally.
chapterbody 7: If my mind inadvertently shifted away, I would pull it back to the little red spot.
chapterbody 8: I was sitting with a mind fully relaxed.
chapterbody 9: I did not contemplate.
chapterbody 10: I did not think.
chapterbody 11: Regardless of the length of time, whether long or short.
chapterbody 12: So I sat. (Without intention)
chapterbody 13: Later, unexpectedly that red spot enlarged, becoming bigger and bigger. The whole room was full of red spots.
chapterbody 14: First each red spot increased one by one, and then more and more appeared until the whole room became red.
chapterbody 16: Finally, even my body fully integrated with the little red spots. I became unified with the red light.
chapterbody 17: I was sitting naturally.
chapterbody 18: I had become the red light.
chapterbody 19: Unexpectedly my body, speech and mind resided in the nature state. All delusions stopped completely, and I entered the state of meditation.
chapterbody 21: The key points are as follows:
chapterbody 22: Body - one should not move.
chapterbody 23: Speech - one should not speak.
chapterbody 24: Mind - one should not think.
chapterbody 26: Oh, heaven! In the state of being half awake and half dreaming, I felt that my mind was very clear.
chapterbody 27: I actually saw the world outside the room. There was a visitor in the living room downstairs, who came to see me.
chapterbody 28: I naturally opened the entrance door, waiting for him.
chapterbody 30: He asked, “How do you know that I am coming?”
chapterbody 31: I replied, “I saw you!”
chapterbody 32: He asked, “How could you see me?”
chapterbody 33: I replied, “One stick of incense.”
chapterbody 34: He was confused!
chapterbody 35: Hahaha!

chaptername: 032 Subtle Cultivation Keys for Light Drops
chapterbody: remember that my guru gave me a crystal ball when I visited him in the past. His intention was for me to begin the practice of light drops.
chapterbody 3: The keys for cultivating light drops is as follows:
chapterbody 4: Visualize the syllable “Om” at one’s ajna chakra.
chapterbody 5: Visualize the syllable “Ah” at one’s throat chakra.
chapterbody 6: Visualize the syllable “Hum” at one’s heart chakra.
chapterbody 7: Visualize the syllable “Ho” at one’s navel chakra.
chapterbody 8: Visualize the syllable “Hang” at one’s sacral chakra.
chapterbody 9: Visualize the syllable “Ha” at one’s crown chakra.
chapterbody 10: Visualize the syllable “Pei” at the tip of one’s root.
chapterbody 12: The key points are as follows:
chapterbody 13: One visualizes the power from the two roots, which ignites the inner fire. One then guides the Descending Qi to enable the inner fire to rise through the central channel to the ajna chakra. As a result, the Bodhicitta Moon Fluid descends. When the light drops descend to the throat chakra, the preliminary bliss occurs. Superior bliss, most superior bliss and innate bliss happen when the light drops descend to the heart chakra, navel chakra and the tip of the Vajra Pestle respectively. (This is the process for the descending of light drops.)
chapterbody 15: Conversely:
chapterbody 16: One experiences four levels of emptiness when the light drops are raised from the tip of the root up to the ajna chakra. One can also feel the four levels of bliss when experiencing the four levels of emptiness.
chapterbody 17: (This is the process for raising the light drops.)
chapterbody 18: When the flow of the light drops is reversed back to the navel chakra, one achieves the homogenous production.
chapterbody 19: When the flow of the light drops is reversed back to the heart chakra, one achieves the result of maturation.
chapterbody 20: When the flow of the light drops is reversed back to the throat chakra, one achieves the results of experience.
chapterbody 21: When the flow of the light drops is reversed back to the brow chakra, one achieves the cessation effect.
chapterbody 23: I would like to state the following:
chapterbody 24: Both the descending and raising process can cause continuous and unlimited bliss. One can experience “the mind and cognition of emptiness” through the feeling of great bliss. This is the most superior attainment. Bliss and emptiness are the foundation of the Buddha’s illusory bodies.
chapterbody 26: The important key points are as follows:
chapterbody 27: Inner fire - the power of the two roots.
chapterbody 28: Qi - the power of circulation.
chapterbody 29: Channels - the power of the paths of transportation.
chapterbody 30: Light drops - the power of cultivation and visualization.
chapterbody 31: Bliss - the power of sensation.
chapterbody 32: Emptiness - the power of realization.
chapterbody 33: The state of Buddhahood is a combination of brilliant light and emptiness.
chapterbody 34: The most refined body consists of the combination of great bliss, brilliant light, and emptiness.
chapterbody 36: Some disciples asked me, “There are nine divisions and four divisions in Vajrayana teachings, which one is correct?”
chapterbody 37: I replied, “Both are correct.”
chapterbody 39: Some disciples have asked, “What do the Father Tantra and the Mother Tantra mean in Vajrayana Buddhism?”
chapterbody 40: I replied, “The Father Tantra represents expedient teachings, while the Mother Tantra represents wisdom.”
chapterbody 42: Some disciples have asked, “What is the difference between Sutrayana and Vajrayana Buddhism?”
chapterbody 43: I answered, “Sutrayana Buddhism is the Bodhisattva Vehicle, which belongs to the Causal Vehicle. Vajrayana Buddhism is the Diamond Vehicle, which is the Resultant Vehicle. These two are inseparable. In Sutrayana Buddhism, one cultivates the cause to attain Buddhahood. In Vajrayana Buddhism, one cultivates the effect to attain Buddhahood. Sutrayana Buddhism is also the most important foundation for cultivating Vajrayana Buddhism.”
chapterbody 45: I would like to tell my disciples:
chapterbody 46: A method that I learned in the past. While when visualizing Bodhicitta Moon Fluid at the brow-point, one must visualize the inner fire burning fiercely.
chapterbody 47: One must take a breath.
chapterbody 48: Thus, the fire expands.
chapterbody 49: Thereby, more light drops will be melted.
chapterbody 50: It is essential to take a breath everytime practicing the step.
chapterbody 51: One’s body will becomes full of white light drops.
chapterbody 52: When one’s body and mind are filled with emptiness, bliss, brilliance and wisdom, one becomes extremely peaceful and happy.
chapterbody 53: One resides in the expansive brilliant light.
chapterbody 56: From the extreme peace and happiness, one enters into a state of deep meditation. At this time, one’s whole body is full of white light and qi.
chapterbody 57: (This is of utmost importance! Utmost importance!)
chapterbody 65: Then, from this light, many small white five-pronged vajra scepters flew out, filled the worlds of the ten directions, and destroyed the four maras of heaven, death, the Five Skandhas, and afflictions.

chapterbody: would like to impart the key to achieve peace of mind based on a personal experience. Through the experience, I entered meditation and underwent the greatest and most incomparable peace and happiness.
chapterbody 3: I visualized the following:
chapterbody 4: Atop my crown chakra were my personal deity Amitabha Buddha and his Buddha Mother perched on a lotus.
chapterbody 5: The stem of the lotus inserted into the crown chakra that connects to my central channel.
chapterbody 6: The Amitabha Buddha and Buddha Mother were in unification.
chapterbody 8: Through the lotus stem and the crown chakra, white nectar filled the central channel.
chapterbody 9: The white nectar filled the five chakras.
chapterbody 10: The white nectar filled the channels and chakras of my whole body.
chapterbody 12: At last, the white nectar filled all the pores of my body.
chapterbody 13: My body was now full of white light drops.
chapterbody 15: During this process, I had been continuously reciting:
chapterbody 16: “Om, a-mee-deh-wah, seh.”
chapterbody 17: I sat in the posture of Amitabha Buddha.
chapterbody 19: The key is:
chapterbody 20: Body – Amitabha’s mudra.
chapterbody 21: Speech – Amitabha’s heart mantra.
chapterbody 22: Mind – Amitabha’s visualization.
chapterbody 23: (Amitabha Buddha and the Buddha Mother in a father-mother form released white nectar that completely filled one’s body.)
chapterbody 24: Thereby, my body, speech and mind became purified.
chapterbody 25: I then entered into a meditation of peace and happiness.
chapterbody 27: At this time, my body transformed into a five-pronged vajra scepter. The top of the five-pronged vajra scepter reached up to the Brahma Heaven, and the bottom down to the earth’s vajra foundation.
chapterbody 28: The five-pronged vajra scepter radiated white light, which spun around the vajra scepter.
chapterbody 29: All evil and enemies were now incapable of harming.
chapterbody 30: Then, from this light, many small white five-pronged vajra scepters flew out, filled the worlds of the ten directions, and destroyed the four maras of heaven, death, the Five Skandhas, and afflictions.
chapterbody 32: Finally, the little white vajras scepters returned back into the large one.
chapterbody 33: I entered into a state of incomparable peace and happiness!
chapterbody 35: Disciples have asked:
chapterbody 36: “Isn’t this kind of peace and happiness achieved by Grand Master Lu just a figment of your imagination?”
chapterbody 37: I answered:
chapterbody 38: “Vajrayana is cultivated expediently by the ritual procedures using both physical and dharma bodies. Through constant practice, the purely visualized physical and dharma bodies can become a reality.”
chapterbody 40: I continued:
chapterbody 41: “All the supreme light, nectar, five-pronged vajra scepter, great bliss sensation, white light, and small vajra scepters are able to be induced through spiritually unified cultivation, especially the blessing and bestowing power from the personal deity. There is no difference between bliss and emptiness in those powers.”
chapterbody 43: The key points for this method are:
chapterbody 44: One cultivates from a coarse body to the most refined body.
chapterbody 45: The illusory body is achieved in this similar way.
chapterbody 46: Brilliant light is also achieved this way.
chapterbody 48: I often give the example:
chapterbody 49: Sunlight,
chapterbody 50: Convex mirror, and

chapterbody 56: Brilliance is manifested.
chapterbody 57: This is the result of induction!
chapterbody 58: I honestly would like to tell everyone that I am not bragging or being boastful!

chaptername: 034 Key Points on Postures (Cultivating One’s Body)
chapterbody: would like to tell a joke first:
chapterbody 2: On a tour bus, a statuesque lady was playing a guessing game.
chapterbody 3: She asked everyone, “Please guess my occupation.”
chapterbody 5: She continued, “Every day, I go to work that requires me to undress. Not only that, I wriggle my waist and shake my buttocks all day long. In addition, I often stretch my legs wide open…”

chapterbody 11: The posture in some Vajrayana analytic meditation practices is essential. Although it does not require practitioners to do any fist movements, the name of the posture is Vajra Fist.
chapterbody 13: The first posture: the Seven-featured Meditation Posture.
chapterbody 14: 1. One’s tongue touches the upper palate to guide the Heart Qi into the central channel.
chapterbody 15: 2. One bends the neck down as a hook to guide the Descending Qi into the central channel. (One’s chin presses on the Adam’s apple.)
chapterbody 16: 3. One maintains a straight spine with open shoulders, so that the Circulating Qi can flow into the central channel.
chapterbody 17: 4. One forms a mudra at the location of the dantian to guide the Balancing Qi to flow into the central channel.
chapterbody 18: 5. One sits cross-legged to guide the Ascending Qi into the central channel.
chapterbody 19: 6. One keeps the mouth closed. No speech is allowed.
chapterbody 20: 7. One stops distracting thoughts and remains focused.
chapterbody 21: (This is the posture of analytic meditation. It is used frequently.)
chapterbody 23: The second posture is known as the Six-fire-pit Mudra Sitting Position.
chapterbody 24: The posture is used for cultivating inner fire. Milarepa was sitting in this posture to successfully accomplish the practice of inner fire.
chapterbody 25: 1. The practitioner sits with both legs crossed, feet flat on the floor, and both of the upper legs pressed against the navel chakra.
chapterbody 26: 2. The practitioner embraces the bent legs with the arms crossed.
chapterbody 27: 3. Throughout this practice, the practitioner must sit upright.
chapterbody 28: (It is easier to initiate the inner fire by using this method.)
chapterbody 29: In this posture, pressing on the navel chakra is the key.
chapterbody 31: The third posture is known as the Vajra Scepter Mudra.
chapterbody 32: This mudra can be formed in either a standing position or a lying down position.
chapterbody 33: Both the positions have a similar effect.
chapterbody 34: The practitioner places his palms together above his head, forming the shape of the upper portion of a vajra scepter.
chapterbody 35: The practitioner keeps his body straight, similar to the middle portion of a vajra scepter.
chapterbody 36: The practitioner bends his legs slightly with both soles of the feet touching, similar to the shape of the lower portion of a vajra scepter.
chapterbody 37: In the standing position, one’s toes must touch the ground supporting one’s weight. This makes this position much more difficult.
chapterbody 38: In the lying down position, it is not as difficult to do.
chapterbody 39: (This mudra transforms the practitioner into a vajra scepter, protecting the practitioner from afflictions and evil obstructions.)
chapterbody 41: The forth posture is known as the Wrathful Subjugation Mudra.
chapterbody 42: The practitioner stands.
chapterbody 43: The practitioner raises his right hand forming the Tarjani Mudra.
chapterbody 44: The practitioner places his left hand in front of his chest.
chapterbody 45: The practitioner makes an angry face.
chapterbody 46: The practitioner stands with his right leg bent and left leg stretching straight out.
chapterbody 47: (This mudra can subjugate the Four Demons.)
chapterbody 49: The fifth is a group of three postures for observing light:
chapterbody 50: The Lion Sitting Posture.
chapterbody 51: The Lying Elephant Posture.
chapterbody 52: The Immortal Sitting Posture.
chapterbody 54: These postures require oral transmissions and demonstrations, and belong to the method of non-leakage.
chapterbody 56: (These three methods require oral transmissions and demonstrations of the postures.)
chapterbody 57: The sixth type of posture consists of the Mudra of Raising the Light Drops Flying to Mount Sumeru, and The Six Sakya Postures.
chapterbody 61: No one can learn all of them, even in one’s life time.
chapterbody 65: The Vajravarahi Twenty-seven Fist Form Practice and One Hundred and Eight Form of Vajra Fist Practice are the practices for guiding qi. The main purpose of the two practices is to assist the circulation of the qi. Guide the qi in the Non-leakage Practice, Inner Fire Practice, and Light Drop Practice with the body mudra.

chapterbody: he secret significance of empowerments in Vajrayana Buddhism is the authorization.
chapterbody 2: Both the method and sequence of receiving empowerments are very important. Vajrayana disciples have to understand the proper method and correct sequence. Do not take them out of order.
chapterbody 3: Empowerments represent lineage.
chapterbody 4: Empowerments represent authorization.
chapterbody 5: Empowerments represent permission.
chapterbody 6: After becoming a disciple in Vajrayana Buddhism, one should receive empowerments from one’s Root Guru, in order to be in accordance with the dharma.
chapterbody 7: Compared with traditional empowerments, modern ones seem less complex.
chapterbody 9: In the past, there were steps before receiving an empowerment.
chapterbody 10: First, one would have to observe the land. Second, one would have to pray to the local earth deity of the land. Third, one would have to purify the land. Fourth, one would have to erect a mandala.
chapterbody 12: Prior to empowerment, there were six types of rituals to follow:
chapterbody 13: First, the earth deity ritual. Second, the five objects ritual. Third, the disciples would have to get prepared. Fourth, bedding would have to be prepared. Fifth, karma threads would have to be prepared. Sixth, auspicious dates would have to be chosen.
chapterbody 15: The succession of the empowerments were as follows:
chapterbody 16: The Treasure Vase Empowerments.
chapterbody 17: The Secret Empowerment.
chapterbody 18: The Wisdom Empowerment.
chapterbody 19: The Perfection Empowerment.
chapterbody 20: The Treasure Vase Empowerments were divided into the Water Empowerment, Personal Deity Empowerment, All Deity Empowerment, Master Empowerment, Dharma Implements Empowerment, Sutra and Tantra Empowerment, Crown Empowerment, Banner Empowerment, Vajra Scepter Empowerment, Vajra Bell Empowerment, Forbidden Empowerment, Title Empowerment, Permission Empowerment, and many more.
chapterbody 21: The Treasure Vase Empowerments belong to the development stage.
chapterbody 23: The other three empowerments belong to the completion stage:
chapterbody 24: The Secret Empowerment. (Internal Practices Empowerment)
chapterbody 25: The Wisdom Empowerment. (Highest Tantra Empowerment)
chapterbody 26: The Perfection Empowerment. (The Foremost Empowerment)
chapterbody 28: I personally feel that the most important key points of empowerments are the following:
chapterbody 29: Inviting and greeting the wisdom deity.
chapterbody 30: The wisdom deity descending to the mandala.
chapterbody 31: Invite the wisdom deity to move from the mandala to the empowerment implement.
chapterbody 32: Carry out the empowerment for the disciples using the implement.
chapterbody 33: These procedures are very important.
chapterbody 35: I remember when I was once in front of the mandala at the Taiwan Lei Tsang Temple. I was intending to transmit the Mahakala empowerment for a disciple.
chapterbody 36: Mahakala descended from the void.
chapterbody 37: Mahakala told me, “There is no Mahakala Mandala!”
chapterbody 38: After confirmation, there was really no Mahakala Mandala, not even a golden statue.
chapterbody 40: Therefore, Mahakala descended into my body.
chapterbody 41: I transformed into Mahakala and carried out the empowerment for the disciple. The retinues of Mahakala descended into my body one by one.
chapterbody 42: (This is a unique situation.)
chapterbody 44: The implements used for empowerments are as follows:
chapterbody 45: The Treasure Vase Empowerments – There are many implements.
chapterbody 46: The Secret Empowerment – Red and white flowers.
chapterbody 47: The Wisdom Empowerment - A pen.
chapterbody 48: The Perfection Empowerment – Secret transmission.
chapterbody 50: I think the meanings in the four levels of empowerments are as follows:
chapterbody 51: Purification of the karma in one’s body.
chapterbody 52: Purification of the karma in one’s speech.
chapterbody 53: The unification of brilliant light and the illusory body.
chapterbody 54: I have always believed that Vajrayana Buddhism rituals are too numerous and complex. No one can learn all of them, even in one’s life time. I have been separating the wheat from the chaff to simplify the rituals as much as possible.
chapterbody 56: Purification of the karma in one’s mind.
chapterbody 58: However, the important procedures cannot be omitted.

chaptername: 036 The Hum Syllable Purification Dharma (Cultivating One’s Speech)
chapterbody: ollowing the teachings of our lineage gurus, practitioners can practice the purification dharma of the Hum syllable. “”
chapterbody 3: The first practice:
chapterbody 4: From the heart, one silently and continuously chants the Hum “” syllable. (Softly)
chapterbody 5: Blue Hum “” syllables are exhaled through the right nostril, one after another.
chapterbody 6: The Hum “” syllables permeate one’s house, houses in the neighborhood, the sky, land, lakes, oceans, mountains and rivers everywhere.
chapterbody 7: While chanting Hum “” syllable continuously, visualize that all the Hum “” syllables return one by one through the left nostril into one’s body.
chapterbody 8: When the whole body is filled with Hum “” syllables from head to toe, one shouts Hum “”.
chapterbody 9: The external and the inner worlds are thereby purified.
chapterbody 11: The second practice:
chapterbody 12: One chants the Hum “” syllable vigorously, visualizing a fierce blue green Hum “” syllable in the heart.
chapterbody 13: The violent Hum “” syllable is released one by one through the left nostril. The fiery, explosive, and thunderous syllables are so powerful like thunder, lightning, and raging fire, that destroy everything in the external world piece by piece with nothing left.
chapterbody 14: (Through the practice, one realizes that the physical world is without self-nature and reality.)
chapterbody 15: The blue-green Hum “” syllables then return to one’s body through the right nostril. The practitioner’s body is full of vigorous Hum “” syllables.
chapterbody 16: With a loud explosion, the practitioner vanishes.
chapterbody 17: The outside world becomes empty.
chapterbody 18: The inside world is also empty.
chapterbody 19: (The practitioner then realizes that neither the outside world nor oneself is real.)
chapterbody 21: The third practice:
chapterbody 22: The practitioner visualizes that there is a red Hum “” syllable outside one’s body.
chapterbody 23: Then the practitioner visualizes their body transforming into a black Hum “” syllable.
chapterbody 24: The two Hum “” syllables grow in size as big as that of Mount Sumeru.
chapterbody 25: The two Hum “” syllables approach each other and intertwine.
chapterbody 26: Then the two Hum “” syllables become as small as a grain of mustard seed, which fly into one’s body through the left and right nostrils, and reside in one’s heart.
chapterbody 27: The practitioner focuses on the red and black Hum “” syllables.
chapterbody 28: The practitioner remains peacefully in the state of Samadhi.
chapterbody 29: (This is a dharma to enter into the state of Samadhi, and abide in gentle meditation)
chapterbody 31: The fourth practice:
chapterbody 32: The practitioner’s body transforms into a white Hum “” syllable.
chapterbody 33: The practitioner’s speech transforms into a white Hum “” syllable.

chaptername: 038 Great Wisdom of Brightness and Emptiness (Cultivating one’s Mind)
chapterbody: first would like to explain the key cultivation points in the four levels of Mahamudra:
chapterbody 2: First, One-Pointedness.
chapterbody 3: Second, Simplicity.
chapterbody 4: Third, One Taste.
chapterbody 5: Fourth, Non-meditation.
chapterbody 6: Sheng-yen Lu
chapterbody 7: What is One-Pointedness?
chapterbody 8: My answer is:
chapterbody 9: “There are no thoughts. Since it appears and disappears naturally, even if there is an idea, it is equivalent to having no idea. One is not attached to or confused by anything. There is only emptiness. This is One-Pointedness.”
chapterbody 11: What is Simplicity?
chapterbody 12: My answer is: “One does not seize, attach oneself to or rest on any thoughts, but responds to everything naturally and open-mindedly. This is Simplicity.”
chapterbody 14: What is One Taste?
chapterbody 15: My answer is: “There is nothing like yours, mine and his, but only equality and non-difference. There are also no so-called achievements or nonachievements. This is One Taste.”
chapterbody 17: What is Non-meditation?
chapterbody 18: My answer is: “There is nothing to pursue. Even if one cultivates, it is equivalent to non-cultivation. Non-cultivation is equal to cultivation. One thoroughly understands the Buddha-nature, and realizes that the Buddha-nature has nothing to do with cultivation or non-cultivation. This is Non-meditation.”
chapterbody 19: (These are important keys.)
chapterbody 21: I ask you:
chapterbody 22: Where are the thoughts coming from?
chapterbody 23: Where do the thoughts stay?
chapterbody 24: Where do the thoughts go?
chapterbody 25: Contemplating firstly on these three questions, one will understand that the mind is free from creation. Then when contemplating on where one’s thoughts stay, one will understand that thoughts have no place to stay. Finally contemplating on how one’s thoughts disappear and where they go, one will understand that thoughts have nowhere to go.
chapterbody 27: Therefore:
chapterbody 28: Thoughts are generated from the emptiness!
chapterbody 29: Thoughts stay in the emptiness!
chapterbody 30: Thoughts disappear to the emptiness!
chapterbody 32: Hence:
chapterbody 33: “There is no so-called creating, staying, and going. In any case one cannot find the thoughts and the mind.”
chapterbody 34: Since one cannot find the thoughts nor the mind, there is only brightness and emptiness.

chapterbody 46: There is no right.

chapterbody 50: No phenomena.

chaptername: 039 Eliminating the Habitual Tendencies of the Six Paths
chapterbody: personally believe and understand that everyone is born with karmic hindrances accumulated from numerous previous lives.
chapterbody 2: It is because many people are born mentally retarded or physically defective, such as being deaf, mute, missing limbs, low IQ, soft bones, crooked mouths, oblique eyes, large heads, short limbs, and etc.
chapterbody 4: These people are just born.
chapterbody 5: They have not committed any good or evil deeds.
chapterbody 6: Why does this occur?
chapterbody 7: This is due to the karmic hindrances accumulated from numerous previous lives! Our habitual tendencies, afflictions, and karmic hindrances are deep-rooted and follow us from our previous lives at birth.
chapterbody 8: Some are accumulated after being born. They are affected by the surrounding environment, and gradually adopt various habitual tendencies.
chapterbody 10: We often say:
chapterbody 11: Dragons give birth to dragons.
chapterbody 12: Phoenixes give birth to phoenixes.
chapterbody 13: Mice are born able to dig holes.
chapterbody 14: Every pickle in the jar tastes the same.
chapterbody 15: This all results from influence by the surrounding environment after birth.
chapterbody 17: Following is the key for the Vajrayana Buddhism Practice of Eliminating the Habits of the Six Paths:
chapterbody 18: The practitioner sits in the Seven-featured Vairocana Posture.
chapterbody 19: The practitioner visualizes the seed syllables corresponding to the Six Paths.
chapterbody 20:  (Ah), is white in color, and located at the ajna chakra corresponding to the Heavenly Path.
chapterbody 21:  (Su), is green in color, and located at the throat chakra corresponding to the Asura Path.
chapterbody 22:  (Ni), is blue in color, and located at the heart chakra corresponding to the Human Path.
chapterbody 23:  (Zhi), is red in color, and located at the navel chakra corresponding to the Animal Path.
chapterbody 24:  (Zhe), is green in color, and located at the sacral chakra corresponding to the Hungry Ghost Path.
chapterbody 25:  (Du), is a smoky color, and located at the bottom of the feet corresponding to the Hell Path.
chapterbody 26: (One should remember that all these seed syllables are pale in color.)
chapterbody 28: The practitioner then visualizes the seed syllables corresponding to the three bodies of a buddha:
chapterbody 29:  (Om), is bright white, and located at the brow chakra.
chapterbody 30:  (Ah), is bright red, and located at the throat chakra.
chapterbody 31:  (Hum), is bright blue, and located at the heart chakra.
chapterbody 32: When these steps are completed, one proceeds to the following steps.
chapterbody 33: One visualizes the three buddha syllables beginning to burn. The three syllables emit fire and spread gradually, until the Six-Path seed syllables are all burned and vanish.
chapterbody 34: The practitioner becomes extremely bright.
chapterbody 35: The practitioner gently chants “Om-ah-hum.”
chapterbody 36: The practitioner continues the chanting until the six seed syllables are completely burned.
chapterbody 37: The more one chants “Om-ah-hum,” the better.
chapterbody 39: If the habitual tendencies of the Six Paths have been completely eliminated, one will have auspicious dreams.
chapterbody 40: 1. The practitioner flies freely in space.
chapterbody 41: 2. The practitioner sees his body being transparent as glass.
chapterbody 42: 3. The practitioner sees white light around his body.
chapterbody 43: 4. The practitioner sees himself wearing white and clean heavenly clothes.
chapterbody 44: 5. The practitioner eats something white and spits out something black.
chapterbody 45: 6. The practitioner takes a shower in white light.
chapterbody 46: 7. The practitioner sees themselves transforming into the personal deity.
chapterbody 47: At this time, the practitioner will feel that their greed, hatred, ignorance, doubt, arrogance, and jealousy are reduced or disappear.
chapterbody 48: Internally, the practitioner is peaceful and unhindered.

chapterbody: enerally, the appearance of bright lights described by a master will include the occurrence of a very short flash of light in daily life. It is difficult to recognize this kind of light. One can only perceive it without any control.
chapterbody 2: Between the moment when a thought has diminished and the next thought has not yet arisen, the bright light occasionally appears.
chapterbody 4: Since infants are not disturbed by delusional thoughts, a baby can often see the bright light.

chapterbody 6: When one has sneezed, one’s mind becomes interrupted and the bright light may flash.
chapterbody 7: When one yawns, one’s mind becomes interrupted and the bright light may flash.
chapterbody 8: When one is in the state of deep sleep, the bright light may flash.
chapterbody 9: When one is dying at the bardo state between death and rebirth, the bright light may flash.
chapterbody 10: When one is knocked unconscious, one’s thoughts cease and the bright light may flash.
chapterbody 11: When one is deeply frightened, one may lose consciousness and the bright light may flash.
chapterbody 12: Among the appearances of the bright light mentioned above, some are meaningless, some cannot be recognized, and all are difficult to grasp.

chapterbody 23: When thoughts are calm, keep them stable.
chapterbody 24: When thoughts are stable, let them expand.
chapterbody 25: In this state of expansion, the bright light is born.
chapterbody 26: (This key may seem to counteract the various thoughts. In fact, one does not have to counter. One just has to realize that the state of disorder is the mind, calmness is the mind, stability is the mind, the state of expansion is the mind, and the bright light is also the mind. It is both bright and empty.)

chaptername: 042 Guard the Enlightenment until It Is Solidified
chapterbody: noticed several practitioners enlightened in theory behave the same as ordinary people. It is equivalent to not being enlightened. Some of them act even worse than unenlightened ones.

chapterbody 4: This occurs because:
chapterbody 5: They do not guard their enlightenment.
chapterbody 6: They do not solidify their enlightenment.
chapterbody 7: Ordinary people are always the slaves to their emotions and desires, whereas the enlightened must not be controlled by their emotions, more importantly not by their desires.
chapterbody 9: For example:

chaptername: 043 Key Points for the Three Kinds of Emptiness
chapterbody: uru Padmasambhava once said:
chapterbody 2: The external world we see, which is manifested through the feelings of our own heart, is intrinsically empty.
chapterbody 3: Our physical body is similar. Being a synthesis of the Four Great Elements, its essence is still fundamentally empty.
chapterbody 4: The consciousness stored inside our physical heart is the Buddhanature, and the Buddha-nature is also emptiness.
chapterbody 5: Many manifestations that we see in actuality are totally a play of the mind. The heart is empty, and therefore the essence of the manifestation is empty.
chapterbody 6: These manifestations are like a play, which will appear and disappear. They are still fundamentally empty.
chapterbody 7: I say, “Empty, empty, empty, empty, empty.”
chapterbody 9: There is a yoga of three kinds of emptiness in Vajrayana Buddhism. Its key points are as follows:
chapterbody 10: One chooses an elevated place to perform the practice, such as a mountain top, the top of a skyscraper, a balcony, or any place that is elevated and quiet, without interference from people walking by.
chapterbody 12: One should sit in either the immortal sitting posture or the Seven Featured Meditation Posture.
chapterbody 13: One should practice the yoga on a sunny, cloudless day that is not windy.
chapterbody 15: The cultivation procedure is as follows:
chapterbody 16: While staring at the cloudless, clear sky, one unifies oneself with the cloudless, clear, wide and spacious sky.
chapterbody 17: The sky is me.
chapterbody 18: I am the sky.
chapterbody 19: One becomes the void.
chapterbody 20: One then looks inwardly to observe one’s own light of absolute eternal wisdom, which becomes illuminating.
chapterbody 21: The absolute eternal wisdom light of Buddha-nature reaches directly to one’s eyes through the Crystal Channel.
chapterbody 22: There is also soft white light inside the Crystal Channel.
chapterbody 24: Then, the external light, light inside the channel, and the absolute eternal wisdom light unify together. One then enters into the bright meditation of the three kinds of emptiness.
chapterbody 26: At this time, one becomes like a drop of water merging into the sea. This is also the unification of the child light with the mother light.
chapterbody 27: In a state of non-meditation, non-rectification, and non-scattering, one gradually increases one’s own light. One has achieved attainment when one reaches complete unification.
chapterbody 29: There are conditions to practice this yoga:
chapterbody 30: 1. The practitioner has opened his heart chakra, and witnessed the absolute eternal wisdom light of the Buddha-nature.
chapterbody 31: 2. The practitioner has witnessed the light of his own channels, the central channel, the three main channels and all the channels of the body including even the Crystal Channel.
chapterbody 32: 3. Only at this time, the unification of external light, the channel lights, and the absolute eternal wisdom light can be realized. Through the unification of the three lights, the practitioner enters into meditation, and achieves the union of the child light with the mother light.
chapterbody 33: (The external light referred to here can be regarded as the cosmic consciousness.)
